abstract_zh: 小型语言模型（SLMs）对于具有严格延迟和计算约束的应用至关重要，但实现高性能仍具挑战性。知识蒸馏（KD）能够从大型教师模型中转移能力，然而现有方法面临两难困境：异策略蒸馏能提供高质量的监督，但存在曝光偏差（训练与推理不匹配）；而同策略方法虽确保了生成一致性，却受限于学生模型自身生成输出质量较低的瓶颈。为应对这些问题，我们提出了AdaSwitch，一种通过自适应切换机制动态融合同策略与异策略生成的新颖方法。AdaSwitch允许学生在自身能力范围内探索预测，并仅在预测差异超过上下文感知阈值时才选择性地整合教师指导。该范式在保持生成一致性的同时确保了高质量监督。在三个数据集上的实验表明，AdaSwitch以适度的计算开销持续提升了模型的准确性与推理能力。

title: Readers Prefer Outputs of AI Trained on Copyrighted Books over Expert Human Writers
authors: Tuhin Chakrabarty, Jane C. Ginsburg, Paramveer Dhillon
abstract: The use of copyrighted books for training AI has sparked lawsuits from authors concerned about AI generating derivative content. Yet whether these models can produce high-quality literary text emulating authors' voices remains unclear. We conducted a preregistered study comparing MFA-trained writers with three frontier models (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) writing up to 450-word excerpts emulating 50 award-winning authors' styles. In blind pairwise evaluations by 28 MFA-trained readers and 516 college-educated general readers, AI text from in-context prompting was strongly disfavored by MFA readers for stylistic fidelity (OR=0.16) and quality (OR=0.13), while general readers showed no fidelity preference (OR=1.06) but favored AI for quality (OR=1.82). Fine-tuning ChatGPT on authors' complete works reversed these results: MFA readers favored AI for fidelity (OR=8.16) and quality (OR=1.87), with general readers showing even stronger preference (fidelity OR=16.65; quality OR=5.42). Both groups preferred fine-tuned AI, but the writer-type X reader-type interaction remained significant (p=0.021 for fidelity; p<10^-4 for quality), indicating general readers favored AI by a wider margin. Effects are robust under cluster-robust inference and generalize across authors in heterogeneity analyses. Fine-tuned outputs were rarely flagged as AI-generated (3% vs. 97% for prompting) by leading detectors. Mediation analysis shows fine-tuning eliminates detectable AI quirks that penalize in-context outputs, altering the nexus between detectability and preference. While not accounting for effort to transform AI output into publishable prose, the median fine-tuning cost of $81 per author represents a 99.7% reduction versus typical writer compensation. Author-specific fine-tuning enables non-verbatim AI writing preferred over expert human writing, providing evidence relevant to copyright's fourth fair-use factor.
reason: 该论文研究AI生成文学文本的读者偏好与版权问题，核心关注点是风格模仿、人类评估与法律公平使用，与您指定的LLM训练技术方向（数据选择、解码优化）无直接关联。论文实验设计严谨、结论清晰，对AI生成内容的社会影响研究有一定参考价值，但不涉及数据筛选、过滤或解码加速等关键技术问题。
abstract_zh: 使用受版权保护的书籍训练人工智能已引发作者们的诉讼，他们担忧AI会生成衍生内容。然而，这些模型能否产出模仿作家风格的高质量文学文本仍不明确。我们开展了一项预注册研究，比较了接受过艺术硕士写作训练的作家与三个前沿模型（ChatGPT、Claude、Gemini）在模仿50位获奖作家风格上创作最多450词节选的表现。在28位接受过艺术硕士训练的读者和516位受过高等教育的普通读者进行的盲法配对评估中，通过上下文提示生成的AI文本在风格忠实度（优势比=0.16）和质量（优势比=0.13）上被艺术硕士读者强烈不偏好；而普通读者在风格上无偏好（优势比=1.06），但在质量上偏好AI（优势比=1.82）。用作者完整作品对ChatGPT进行微调后，结果发生逆转：艺术硕士读者在风格（优势比=8.16）和质量（优势比=1.87）上均偏好AI；普通读者的偏好更为强烈（风格优势比=16.65；质量优势比=5.42）。两组均偏好微调后的AI，但作家类型与读者类型的交互作用在风格（p=0.021）和质量（p<10^-4）上均显著，表明普通读者对AI的偏好幅度更大。聚类稳健推断下效应稳健，异质性分析表明结果可推广至不同作者。微调后的输出被主流检测器判定为AI生成的比例极低（3%，而提示词工程为97%）。中介分析表明，微调消除了导致上下文提示输出被检测并产生负面评价的AI特征，从而改变了可检测性与偏好之间的关系。本研究未考虑将AI输出转化为可出版散文所需的人力，但每作者微调的中位成本仅81美元，相较于典型作家报酬降低了99.7%。针对特定作者的微调能够实现非逐字照搬的AI写作，其偏好度超过专业人类写作，为著作权法中的“合理使用”第四要素提供了相关证据。

title: Surfacing Subtle Stereotypes: A Multilingual, Debate-Oriented Evaluation of Modern LLMs
authors: Muhammed Saeed, Muhammad Abdul-mageed, Shady Shehata
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are widely deployed for open-ended communication, yet most bias evaluations still rely on English, classification-style tasks. We introduce \corpusname, a new multilingual, debate-style benchmark designed to reveal how narrative bias appears in realistic generative settings. Our dataset includes 8{,}400 structured debate prompts spanning four sensitive domains -- Women's Rights, Backwardness, Terrorism, and Religion -- across seven languages ranging from high-resource (English, Chinese) to low-resource (Swahili, Nigerian Pidgin). Using four flagship models (GPT-4o, Claude~3.5~Haiku, DeepSeek-Chat, and LLaMA-3-70B), we generate over 100{,}000 debate responses and automatically classify which demographic groups are assigned stereotyped versus modern roles. Results show that all models reproduce entrenched stereotypes despite safety alignment: Arabs are overwhelmingly linked to Terrorism and Religion ($\geq$89\%), Africans to socioeconomic ``backwardness'' (up to 77\%), and Western groups are consistently framed as modern or progressive. Biases grow sharply in lower-resource languages, revealing that alignment trained primarily in English does not generalize globally. Our findings highlight a persistent divide in multilingual fairness: current alignment methods reduce explicit toxicity but fail to prevent biased outputs in open-ended contexts. We release our \corpusname benchmark and analysis framework to support the next generation of multilingual bias evaluation and safer, culturally inclusive model alignment.
reason: 论文聚焦于LLM偏见的多语言评估，与用户的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择和推测解码）无直接关联。论文本身质量较高，但属于AI伦理范畴，对用户的技术研究参考价值有限。
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）被广泛用于开放式交流，但大多数偏见评估仍依赖于英文、分类式任务。我们引入了\corpusname，这是一个新的多语言、辩论式基准测试，旨在揭示叙事偏见在真实生成场景中如何显现。我们的数据集包含8,400个结构化辩论提示，涵盖四个敏感领域——女性权利、落后性、恐怖主义和宗教——横跨七种语言，从高资源语言（英语、中文）到低资源语言（斯瓦希里语、尼日利亚皮钦语）。使用四个旗舰模型（GPT-4o、Claude 3.5 Haiku、DeepSeek-Chat和LLaMA-3-70B），我们生成了超过100,000个辩论回复，并自动分类哪些群体被分配了刻板印象与现代角色。结果显示，尽管进行了安全对齐，所有模型都再现了根深蒂固的刻板印象：阿拉伯人绝大多数与恐怖主义和宗教相关（≥89%），非洲人与社会经济“落后”相关（高达77%），西方群体 consistently 被描绘为现代或进步。偏见在低资源语言中急剧增长，揭示了主要在英语中训练的对齐并未在全球范围内通用。我们的发现突显了多语言公平性中的持续差距：当前的对齐方法减少了显式毒性，但未能防止开放式语境中的偏见输出。我们发布了\corpusname基准和分析框架，以支持下一代多语言偏见评估以及更安全、文化包容的模型对齐。
core_contribution: 为解决现有LLM偏见评估过度依赖英文封闭式分类任务、难以捕捉真实生成场景中叙事偏见的问题，该研究构建了一个覆盖多语言、多敏感领域的辩论式生成基准（\corpusname），并系统性揭示了主流模型在跨语言语境中再现刻板印象的严重性及其与资源多寡的相关性。

title: Steering LLMs toward Korean Local Speech: Iterative Refinement Framework for Faithful Dialect Translation
authors: Keunhyeung Park, Seunguk Yu, Youngbin Kim
abstract: Standard-to-dialect machine translation remains challenging due to a persistent dialect gap in large language models and evaluation distortions inherent in n-gram metrics, which favor source copying over authentic dialect translation. In this paper, we propose the dialect refinement (DIA-REFINE) framework, which guides LLMs toward faithful target dialect outputs through an iterative loop of translation, verification, and feedback using external dialect classifiers. To address the limitations of n-gram-based metrics, we introduce the dialect fidelity score (DFS) to quantify linguistic shift and the target dialect ratio (TDR) to measure the success of dialect translation. Experiments on Korean dialects across zero-shot and in-context learning baselines demonstrate that DIA-REFINE consistently enhances dialect fidelity. The proposed metrics distinguish between False Success cases, where high n-gram scores obscure failures in dialectal translation, and True Attempt cases, where genuine attempts at dialectal translation yield low n-gram scores. We also observed that models exhibit varying degrees of responsiveness to the framework, and that integrating in-context examples further improves the translation of dialectal expressions. Our work establishes a robust framework for goal-directed, inclusive dialect translation, providing both rigorous evaluation and critical insights into model performance.
reason: 该论文的研究内容（方言翻译框架与评估指标）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在核心目标、方法论和关键词上均无直接重叠。论文本身质量较高，问题定义清晰，提出了创新的迭代框架和针对性评估指标，并在实验上进行了验证。然而，其应用场景（方言翻译）和核心技术（基于外部分类器的迭代微调/提示优化）不属于用户关注的LLM训练数据工程或解码加速范畴，因此相关性很低。但因其在LLM应用与评估方面仍具一般性参考价值，故评为2分。
abstract_zh: 标准语到方言的机器翻译仍面临挑战，原因在于大语言模型中存在持续的方言差距，以及n-gram指标固有的评估偏差——这类指标倾向于偏好源文本复制而非真实的方言翻译。本文提出方言精炼（DIA-REFINE）框架，通过利用外部方言分类器进行翻译、验证与反馈的迭代循环，引导大语言模型生成忠实的目标方言输出。为克服基于n-gram指标的局限性，我们引入了方言忠实度分数（DFS）以量化语言转换程度，并采用目标方言比率（TDR）来衡量方言翻译的成功率。在韩语方言上针对零样本与上下文学习基线的实验表明，DIA-REFINE能持续提升方言忠实度。所提指标能够区分“伪成功”案例（高n-gram分数掩盖了方言翻译的失败）与“真尝试”案例（真正的方言翻译尝试却获得低n-gram分数）。我们还观察到，不同模型对该框架的响应程度存在差异，且融入上下文示例能进一步提升方言表达的翻译质量。本研究为面向目标、包容性方言翻译建立了稳健框架，同时提供了严格的评估方法及对模型性能的关键洞察。

title: Toward Better Temporal Structures for Geopolitical Events Forecasting
authors: Kian Ahrabian, Eric Boxer, Jay Pujara
abstract: Forecasting on geopolitical temporal knowledge graphs (TKGs) through the lens of large language models (LLMs) has recently gained traction. While TKGs and their generalization, hyper-relational temporal knowledge graphs (HTKGs), offer a straightforward structure to represent simple temporal relationships, they lack the expressive power to convey complex facts efficiently. One of the critical limitations of HTKGs is a lack of support for more than two primary entities in temporal facts, which commonly occur in real-world events. To address this limitation, in this work, we study a generalization of HTKGs, Hyper-Relational Temporal Knowledge Generalized Hypergraphs (HTKGHs). We first derive a formalization for HTKGHs, demonstrating their backward compatibility while supporting two complex types of facts commonly found in geopolitical incidents. Then, utilizing this formalization, we introduce the htkgh-polecat dataset, built upon the global event database POLECAT. Finally, we benchmark and analyze popular LLMs on our dataset, providing insights into 1) the positive impact of utilizing the HTKGH formalization compared to existing ones and 2) LLMs' adaptability and capabilities in complex forecasting tasks.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是提出了一种新的知识表示结构（HTKGHs）及其对应的数据集，并评估了LLMs在该结构上的表现。其研究重点在于知识图谱的表示学习与复杂事件预测，与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在技术焦点上无直接关联。论文虽使用了LLM，但未涉及数据筛选、过滤、去重或解码加速等用户关心的具体问题。因此，相关性较低，但其在知识表示与事件预测方面的系统研究对理解LLM在复杂结构化任务中的能力具有一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型对地缘政治时序知识图进行预测的研究近来备受关注。尽管时序知识图及其泛化形式——超关系时序知识图（HTKGs）——为表达简单时序关系提供了直接的结构，但它们在高效传达复杂事实时表达能力有限。HTKGs的一个关键局限在于无法支持时序事实时涉及两个以上的主要实体，而这在真实世界事件中极为常见。为克服此局限，本研究探讨了HTKGs的一种泛化形式——超关系时序知识广义超图（HTKGHs）。我们首先为HTKGHs建立了形式化定义，证明其向后兼容性，同时支持地缘政治事件中常见的两种复杂事实验证。随后，基于此形式化定义，我们构建了源自全球事件数据库POLECAT的htkgh-polecat数据集。最后，我们在该数据集上对主流大语言模型进行了基准测试与分析，揭示了：1）相较于现有形式，采用HTKGH形式化定义的积极影响；2）大语言模型在复杂预测任务中的适应性与能力表现。

title: LLM-Augmented Changepoint Detection: A Framework for Ensemble Detection and Automated Explanation
authors: Fabian Lukassen, Christoph Weisser, Michael Schlee, Manish Kumar, Anton Thielmann, Benjamin Saefken, Thomas Kneib
abstract: This paper introduces a novel changepoint detection framework that combines ensemble statistical methods with Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance both detection accuracy and the interpretability of regime changes in time series data. Two critical limitations in the field are addressed. First, individual detection methods exhibit complementary strengths and weaknesses depending on data characteristics, making method selection non-trivial and prone to suboptimal results. Second, automated, contextual explanations for detected changes are largely absent. The proposed ensemble method aggregates results from ten distinct changepoint detection algorithms, achieving superior performance and robustness compared to individual methods. Additionally, an LLM-powered explanation pipeline automatically generates contextual narratives, linking detected changepoints to potential real-world historical events. For private or domain-specific data, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) solution enables explanations grounded in user-provided documents. The open source Python framework demonstrates practical utility in diverse domains, including finance, political science, and environmental science, transforming raw statistical output into actionable insights for analysts and decision-makers.
reason: 该论文的核心是使用LLM为变点检测结果生成解释，并将其应用于时间序列分析领域。论文的重点在于LLM作为下游任务（解释生成）的工具，而非研究用户指定的两个核心方向：1) LLM训练中的数据选择（如数据筛选、课程学习等）；2) 推测解码技术。文中未涉及训练数据的处理、优化或解码加速等关键词，与用户研究方向相关性很低。

title: SentGraph: Hierarchical Sentence Graph for Multi-hop Retrieval-Augmented Question Answering
authors: Junli Liang, Pengfei Zhou, Wangqiu Zhou, Wenjie Qing, Qi Zhao, Ziwen Wang, Qi Song, Xiangyang Li
abstract: Traditional Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) effectively supports single-hop question answering with large language models but faces significant limitations in multi-hop question answering tasks, which require combining evidence from multiple documents. Existing chunk-based retrieval often provides irrelevant and logically incoherent context, leading to incomplete evidence chains and incorrect reasoning during answer generation. To address these challenges, we propose SentGraph, a sentence-level graph-based RAG framework that explicitly models fine-grained logical relationships between sentences for multi-hop question answering. Specifically, we construct a hierarchical sentence graph offline by first adapting Rhetorical Structure Theory to distinguish nucleus and satellite sentences, and then organizing them into topic-level subgraphs with cross-document entity bridges. During online retrieval, SentGraph performs graph-guided evidence selection and path expansion to retrieve fine-grained sentence-level evidence. Extensive experiments on four multi-hop question answering benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of SentGraph, validating the importance of explicitly modeling sentence-level logical dependencies for multi-hop reasoning.
reason: 该论文的主题是设计一个用于多跳问答的图结构检索增强生成框架（SentGraph），核心贡献在于通过句子级图建模改善证据链的连贯性。这与用户指定的两个研究方向——LLM训练中的数据选择（如数据清洗、筛选、课程学习）和推测解码（如加速推理的采样技术）——在技术目标、应用场景和方法论上均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据质量评估、数据过滤策略，也未讨论解码过程优化或推理加速。因此，与用户研究方向的关联度极低，对用户的研究工作参考价值有限。

title: Multilingual, Multimodal Pipeline for Creating Authentic and Structured Fact-Checked Claim Dataset
authors: Z. Melce H\"us\"unbeyi, Virginie Mouilleron, Leonie Uhling, Daniel Foppe, Tatjana Scheffler, Djam\'e Seddah
abstract: The rapid proliferation of misinformation across online platforms underscores the urgent need for robust, up-to-date, explainable, and multilingual fact-checking resources. However, existing datasets are limited in scope, often lacking multimodal evidence, structured annotations, and detailed links between claims, evidence, and verdicts. This paper introduces a comprehensive data collection and processing pipeline that constructs multimodal fact-checking datasets in French and German languages by aggregating ClaimReview feeds, scraping full debunking articles, normalizing heterogeneous claim verdicts, and enriching them with structured metadata and aligned visual content. We used state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs) and multimodal LLMs for (i) evidence extraction under predefined evidence categories and (ii) justification generation that links evidence to verdicts. Evaluation with G-Eval and human assessment demonstrates that our pipeline enables fine-grained comparison of fact-checking practices across different organizations or media markets, facilitates the development of more interpretable and evidence-grounded fact-checking models, and lays the groundwork for future research on multilingual, multimodal misinformation verification.
reason: 该论文的核心工作是构建一个用于事实验证的多语言、多模态数据集及处理流程，并利用LLM进行证据提取和理由生成。虽然论文中使用了LLM，但其研究目标（数据集构建）与方法（证据提取、理由生成）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均无直接关联。论文本身设计系统、有评估，在事实验证领域具有一定价值，但不符合用户关注的技术焦点。
abstract_zh: 错误信息在在线平台上的迅速蔓延，凸显出对稳健、最新、可解释且多语言的事实核查资源的迫切需求。然而，现有数据集在覆盖范围上存在局限，通常缺乏多模态证据、结构化标注，以及声明、证据与裁决之间的详细关联。本文介绍了一个全面的数据收集与处理流程，该流程通过聚合ClaimReview信息源、抓取完整的辟谣文章、规范化异构的声明裁决，并为其添加结构化元数据和对齐的视觉内容，构建了法语和德语的多模态事实核查数据集。我们使用了最先进的大型语言模型（LLMs）和多模态LLM，用于(i)在预定义证据类别下进行证据提取，以及(ii)生成将证据与裁决联系起来的理由。通过G-Eval和人工评估表明，我们的流程能够实现不同机构或媒体市场间事实核查实践的细粒度比较，促进开发更具可解释性、基于证据的事实核查模型，并为未来多语言、多模态错误信息验证研究奠定基础。

title: From Intuition to Calibrated Judgment: A Rubric-Based Expert-Panel Study of Human Detection of LLM-Generated Korean Text
authors: Shinwoo Park, Yo-Sub Han
abstract: Distinguishing human-written Korean text from fluent LLM outputs remains difficult even for trained readers, who can over-trust surface well-formedness. We present LREAD, a Korean-specific instantiation of a rubric-based expert-calibration framework for human attribution of LLM-generated text. In a three-phase blind longitudinal study with three linguistically trained annotators, Phase 1 measures intuition-only attribution, Phase 2 introduces criterion-anchored scoring with explicit justifications, and Phase 3 evaluates a limited held-out elementary-persona subset. Majority-vote accuracy improves from 0.60 in Phase 1 to 0.90 in Phase 2, and reaches 10/10 on the limited Phase 3 subset (95% CI [0.692, 1.000]); agreement also increases from Fleiss' $\kappa$ = -0.09 to 0.82. Error analysis suggests that calibration primarily reduces false negatives on AI essays rather than inducing generalized over-detection. We position LREAD as pilot evidence for within-panel calibration in a Korean argumentative-essay setting. These findings suggest that rubric-scaffolded human judgment can complement automated detectors by making attribution reasoning explicit, auditable, and adaptable. The rubric developed in this study, along with the dataset employed for the analysis, is available at https://github.com/Shinwoo-Park/lread.

title: GroupGPT: A Token-efficient and Privacy-preserving Agentic Framework for Multi-User Chat Assistant
authors: Zhuokang Shen, Yifan Wang, Hanyu Chen, Wenxuan Huang, Yunhang Shen, Shaohui Lin
abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled increasingly capable chatbots. However, most existing systems focus on single-user settings and do not generalize well to multi-user group chats, where agents require more proactive and accurate intervention under complex, evolving contexts. Existing approaches typically rely on LLMs for both reasoning and generation, leading to high token consumption, limited scalability, and potential privacy risks. To address these challenges, we propose GroupGPT, a token-efficient and privacy-preserving agentic framework for multi-user chat assistant. GroupGPT adopts a small-large model collaborative architecture to decouple intervention timing from response generation, enabling efficient and accurate decision-making. The framework also supports multimodal inputs, including memes, images, videos, and voice messages. We further introduce MUIR, a benchmark dataset for multi-user chat assistant intervention reasoning. MUIR contains 2,500 annotated group chat segments with intervention labels and rationales, supporting evaluation of timing accuracy and response quality. We evaluate a range of models on MUIR, from large language models to smaller counterparts. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GroupGPT produces accurate and well-timed responses, achieving an average score of 4.72/5.0 in LLM-based evaluation, and is well received by users across diverse group chat scenarios. Moreover, GroupGPT reduces token usage by up to 3 times compared to baseline methods, while providing privacy sanitization of user messages before cloud transmission. Code is available at: https://github.com/Eliot-Shen/GroupGPT .

abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的最新进展使聊天机器人能力日益增强。然而，现有系统大多专注于单用户场景，难以泛化到多用户群聊环境——在复杂且动态变化的对话中，智能体需要更主动、更精准的干预。当前方法通常依赖LLMs同时完成推理与生成，导致token消耗高、可扩展性有限，并存在隐私风险。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了GroupGPT，一个面向多用户聊天助手的token高效且隐私保护的智能体框架。GroupGPT采用小模型与大模型协同的架构，将干预时机判断与回复生成解耦，从而实现高效准确的决策。该框架还支持多模态输入，包括表情包、图片、视频和语音消息。我们进一步引入了MUIR，一个用于多用户聊天助手干预推理的基准数据集。MUIR包含2500个带标注的群聊片段，提供干预标签与推理依据，支持对时机准确性与回复质量进行评估。我们在MUIR上评估了从大型到小型的一系列模型。大量实验表明，GroupGPT能产生准确且时机恰当的回复，在基于LLM的评估中平均得分达4.72/5.0，并在多样化的群聊场景中受到用户好评。此外，GroupGPT相比基线方法最多减少3倍的token使用，并在消息上传云端前进行隐私净化处理。代码已开源：https://github.com/Eliot-Shen/GroupGPT

title: A Survey of Frontiers in LLM Reasoning: Inference Scaling, Learning to Reason, and Agentic Systems
authors: Zixuan Ke, Fangkai Jiao, Yifei Ming, Xuan-Phi Nguyen, Austin Xu, Do Xuan Long, Minzhi Li, Chengwei Qin, Peifeng Wang, Silvio Savarese, Caiming Xiong, Shafiq Joty
abstract: Reasoning is a fundamental cognitive process that enables logical inference, problem-solving, and decision-making. With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), reasoning has emerged as a key capability that distinguishes advanced AI systems from conventional models that empower chatbots. In this survey, we categorize existing methods along two orthogonal dimensions: (1) Regimes, which define the stage at which reasoning is achieved (either at inference time or through dedicated training); and (2) Architectures, which determine the components involved in the reasoning process, distinguishing between standalone LLMs and agentic compound systems that incorporate external tools, and multi-agent collaborations. Within each dimension, we analyze two key perspectives: (1) Input level, which focuses on techniques that construct high-quality prompts that the LLM condition on; and (2) Output level, which methods that refine multiple sampled candidates to enhance reasoning quality. This categorization provides a systematic understanding of the evolving landscape of LLM reasoning, highlighting emerging trends such as the shift from inference-scaling to learning-to-reason (e.g., DeepSeek-R1), and the transition to agentic workflows (e.g., OpenAI Deep Research, Manus Agent). Additionally, we cover a broad spectrum of learning algorithms, from supervised fine-tuning to reinforcement learning such as PPO and GRPO, and the training of reasoners and verifiers. We also examine key designs of agentic workflows, from established patterns like generator-evaluator and LLM debate to recent innovations. ...

abstract_zh: 推理是一种基础认知过程，它支持逻辑推断、问题求解与决策制定。随着大语言模型（LLMs）的飞速发展，推理已成为区分先进AI系统与传统赋能聊天机器人模型的关键能力。在本综述中，我们沿两个正交维度对现有方法进行分类：（1）阶段，定义了实现推理的时机（或在推理时，或通过专门训练）；（2）架构，决定了参与推理过程的组件，区分独立运行的LLM与集成外部工具的智能体复合系统，以及多智能体协作。在每个维度内，我们分析两个关键视角：（1）输入层面，侧重于构建LLM所需的高质量提示词的技术；（2）输出层面，关注通过精炼多个采样候选输出来提升推理质量的方法。此分类提供了对LLM推理演进格局的系统理解，凸显了从推理缩放到学习推理（例如DeepSeek-R1）的新兴趋势，以及向智能体工作流（例如OpenAI Deep Research、Manus Agent）的转变。此外，我们覆盖了广泛的算法，从监督微调到强化学习（如PPO和GRPO），以及推理器和验证器的训练。我们还考察了智能体工作流的关键设计，从生成器-评估器和LLM辩论等成熟模式到近期创新……

title: Learning to Diagnose Privately: DP-Powered LLMs for Radiology Report Classification
authors: Payel Bhattacharjee, Fengwei Tian, Geoffrey D. Rubin, Joseph Y. Lo, Nirav Merchant, Heidi Hanson, John Gounley, Ravi Tandon
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted across domains such as education, healthcare, and finance. In healthcare, LLMs support tasks including disease diagnosis, abnormality classification, and clinical decision-making. Among these, multi-abnormality classification of radiology reports is critical for clinical workflow automation and biomedical research. Leveraging strong natural language processing capabilities, LLMs enable efficient processing of unstructured medical text and reduce the administrative burden of manual report analysis. To improve performance, LLMs are often fine-tuned on private, institution-specific datasets such as radiology reports. However, this raises significant privacy concerns: LLMs may memorize training data and become vulnerable to data extraction attacks, while sharing fine-tuned models risks exposing sensitive patient information. Despite growing interest in LLMs for medical text classification, privacy-preserving fine-tuning for multi-abnormality classification remains underexplored. To address this gap, we propose a differentially private (DP) fine-tuning framework for multi-abnormality classification from free-text radiology reports. Our approach integrates differential privacy with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to efficiently fine-tune LLMs on sensitive clinical data while mitigating leakage risks. We further employ labels generated by a larger LLM to train smaller models, enabling efficient inference under strong privacy guarantees. Experiments on MIMIC-CXR and CT-RATE demonstrate the effectiveness of our DP-LoRA framework across varying privacy regimes. On MIMIC-CXR, our method achieves weighted F1-scores up to 0.89 under moderate privacy budgets, approaching non-private LoRA (0.90) and full fine-tuning (0.96), confirming that strong privacy can be achieved with only modest performance trade-offs.

abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正日益被应用于教育、医疗保健和金融等领域。在医疗领域，LLMs 支持包括疾病诊断、异常分类和临床决策在内的多项任务。其中，放射学报告的多异常分类对于临床工作流自动化和生物医学研究至关重要。LLMs 凭借其强大的自然语言处理能力，能够高效处理非结构化医学文本，并减轻人工报告分析的管理负担。为提升性能，LLMs 常需在放射学报告等私有、机构特定的数据集上进行微调。然而，这引发了严重的隐私担忧：LLMs 可能记住训练数据，从而易受数据提取攻击；共享微调后的模型也存在暴露敏感患者信息的风险。尽管人们对将 LLMs 应用于医学文本分类的兴趣日益浓厚，但针对多异常分类的隐私保护微调方法仍未得到充分探索。为填补这一空白，我们提出了一种基于差分隐私（DP）的框架，用于从自由文本放射学报告中进行多异常分类。该方法将差分隐私与低秩适配（LoRA）相结合，可在敏感临床数据上高效微调 LLMs，同时降低信息泄露风险。我们还利用一个更大型 LLM 生成的标签来训练更小的模型，从而在强隐私保障下实现高效推理。在 MIMIC-CXR 和 CT-RATE 数据集上的实验表明，我们提出的 DP-LoRA 框架在不同隐私约束下均有效。在 MIMIC-CXR 上，我们的方法在中等隐私预算下达到了高达 0.89 的加权 F1 分数，接近非私有 LoRA（0.90）和全量微调（0.96）的结果，证实了在仅有微小性能损失的前提下可实现强隐私保护。

title: TempCore: Are Video QA Benchmarks Temporally Grounded? A Frame Selection Sensitivity Analysis and Benchmark
authors: Hyunjong Ok, Jaeho Lee
abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) can ingest only a limited number of video frames, making frame selection a practical necessity. But do current Video QA benchmarks genuinely require temporal frame selection, or can most questions be answered regardless of which frames are shown? We introduce Frame Selection Sensitivity (FSS), a per-sample diagnostic that measures how much VLM accuracy changes when the most relevant frames are replaced with the least relevant ones. Across six benchmarks and eight VLMs, we find that a large majority of samples are frame-agnostic: only a minority are genuinely sensitive to frame choice. Combining FSS with a Language Independence Score (LIS) reveals that merely 8--33% of samples are Temporally Sensitive. We construct TempCore, compact evaluation subsets that isolate these temporal samples from existing benchmarks, and will release code and per-sample annotations upon publication.
reason: 该论文的核心研究问题——如何评估和选择对模型性能最关键的数据子集（在此具体为视频帧）——与您研究方向中的‘llm-data-selection’（数据选择、数据质量、影响函数等）高度契合。论文提出了新颖的‘帧选择敏感性’诊断方法，并在多个基准和模型上进行了大规模实证分析，发现了现有视频QA基准中大量样本对帧选择不敏感的重要现象，并据此构建了更严谨的评估子集。这项工作方法论严谨、实验充分、结论对领域有重要启示，其关于数据敏感性与质量的洞察可直接迁移或启发LLM训练中的数据筛选与质量控制研究，因此属于高度相关且质量极高的论文，值得精读和深入分析。
abstract_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）一次只能处理有限数量的视频帧，这使得帧选择成为一个实际必要步骤。但当前的视频问答（Video QA）基准测试是否真的需要时间上的帧选择？还是说大多数问题无论显示哪几帧都能回答？我们引入了帧选择敏感性（FSS），一种针对每个样本的诊断方法，用于衡量当最相关的帧被替换为最不相关的帧时，VLM的准确率变化程度。在六个基准测试和八个VLMs上，我们发现绝大多数样本是“帧无关”的：只有少数样本真正对帧的选择敏感。将FSS与语言独立性分数（LIS）相结合，我们发现仅有8%-33%的样本是“时间敏感”的。我们构建了TempCore，这是从现有基准中分离出这些时间敏感样本的紧凑评估子集，并将在发表后发布代码和每样本标注。
method_highlights: 提出帧选择敏感性（FSS）指标：通过系统性地用‘最不相关帧’替换‘最相关帧’并测量模型性能下降，直接量化单个样本对帧选择的依赖程度，这是一种精细化的、样本级别的诊断方法。
设计语言独立性分数（LIS）：通过将视频输入替换为纯文本描述（或反之）来测试模型是否过度依赖单一模态，从而与FSS结合，有效过滤掉仅因语言偏见或视觉表面线索而看似敏感的问题，精准识别真正需要时间推理的样本。
构建TempCore核心原则：不是创建新数据，而是从现有大规模基准中自动筛选出FSS高且LIS低的‘时间敏感’样本子集，提供了一个纯净、高效的评估工具，解决了现有基准信噪比低的问题。
experiment_highlights: 广泛验证：在ActivityNet、MSRVTT、TGIF、How2QA、MSVD-QA、TVQA六个主流Video QA基准及GPT-4V、Video-LLaMA等八个代表性VLMs上进行实验，结论具有普遍性。
核心发现：大多数（超过67%）Video QA样本是‘帧无关’或‘语言依赖’的，模型性能对随机帧采样不敏感，或仅依赖问题文本即可回答，揭示现有基准可能高估了模型的时间理解能力。
TempCore有效性：所构建的TempCore子集能显著区分模型的时间理解能力，在TempCore上性能下降更明显的模型，通常拥有更强的细粒度时间推理设计，证明了其作为诊断基准的价值。
比例量化：不同基准中真正‘时间敏感’样本的比例仅为8%-33%，差异显著，说明各基准的时间理解要求程度不同。
pros: 诊断视角新颖：将焦点从‘模型整体性能’转向‘样本级时间依赖性’，揭示了Video QA领域长期被忽视的基准质量问题，具有重要的纠偏意义。
方法设计巧妙：FSS与LIS的结合形成了一套逻辑自洽的过滤器，能有效分离‘真·时间理解’与‘伪·时间敏感’，方法论严谨且可解释性强。
实用价值高：发布的TempCore子集和逐样本标注，为社区提供了立即可用的、更纯净的评估工具，能推动更可靠的时间视觉-语言模型研发。
结论具有冲击力：用大量实验证据表明，当前主流Video QA任务可能主要考验静态图像理解或语言推理，而非视频特有的时间动态理解，这一结论将引发领域深思。
cons: ‘最相关/最不相关帧’的获取依赖：FSS计算需要预定义一个帧相关性排序（如通过一个预训练模型或人工标注），该排序的质量直接影响FSS的可靠性，而论文中使用的自动方法可能并非最优。
对‘时间敏感’的定义可能偏严格：当前定义（高FSS+低LIS）可能排除了那些需要结合时间线索和语言线索的合理问题，或许存在‘灰区’样本未被充分考虑。
模型覆盖与泛化性：实验虽涉及8个VLMs，但领域发展迅速，结论是否适用于最新的大型多模态模型（如Gemini、Sora等）有待验证。
TempCore的规模与多样性：作为‘紧凑子集’，其样本量较小，可能无法完全代表原基准的全部数据分布和任务类型，在全面评估时需谨慎。
reproducibility: 高可复现性。论文明确声明将公开代码和所有样本的FSS/LIS评分及TempCore子集列表，这为完全复现提供了基础。实验部分详细描述了使用的六个基准数据集、八个具体模型版本、帧采样策略（如均匀采样）、FSS计算的具体流程（包括用于帧排序的CLIP模型及提示工程）以及LIS的计算方式，参数设置清晰。但需注意，部分依赖于特定预训练模型（如用于帧排序的CLIP）的结果，可能受其版本和随机性的轻微影响。
task_list: 阅读并理解论文中FSS和LIS的数学定义与计算流程，尝试在另一个小众Video QA数据集（如VidQA）上复现该分析流程。
使用发布的TempCore子集，对当前最先进的视频大模型（如Video-LLaMA 2, GPT-4o）进行零样本/小样本评估，验证其区分度。
深入分析TempCore中被筛选出的‘时间敏感’样本的共性特征（如问题类型、涉及的动作类型、时间跨度），形成定性报告。
设计一个简单的基线模型，其核心是显式地基于FSS分数进行帧选择（而非均匀采样），评估其在TempCore和全基准上的表现，验证帧选择策略的有效性。
探索将LIS思想扩展到其他模态偏见诊断中，例如在音频-视频任务中设计‘音频独立性分数’。

title: ReasoningBank: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
authors: Siru Ouyang, Jun Yan, I-Hung Hsu, Yanfei Chen, Ke Jiang, Zifeng Wang, Rujun Han, Long T. Le, Samira Daruki, Xiangru Tang, Vishy Tirumalashetty, George Lee, Mahsan Rofouei, Hangfei Lin, Jiawei Han, Chen-Yu Lee, Tomas Pfister
abstract: With the growing adoption of large language model agents in persistent real-world roles, they naturally encounter continuous streams of tasks. A key limitation, however, is their failure to learn from the accumulated interaction history, forcing them to discard valuable insights and repeat past errors. We propose ReasoningBank, a novel memory framework that distills generalizable reasoning strategies from an agent's self-judged successful and failed experiences. At test time, an agent retrieves relevant memories from ReasoningBank to inform its interaction and then integrates new learnings back, enabling it to become more capable over time. Building on this powerful experience learner, we further introduce memory-aware test-time scaling (MaTTS), which accelerates and diversifies this learning process by scaling up the agent's interaction experience. By allocating more compute to each task, the agent generates abundant, diverse experiences that provide rich contrastive signals for synthesizing higher-quality memory. The better memory in turn guides more effective scaling, establishing a powerful synergy between memory and test-time scaling. Across web browsing and software engineering benchmarks, ReasoningBank consistently outperforms existing memory mechanisms that store raw trajectories or only successful task routines, improving both effectiveness and efficiency; MaTTS further amplifies these gains. These findings establish memory-driven experience scaling as a new scaling dimension, enabling agents to self-evolve with emergent behaviors naturally arise. Our code can be found at https://github.com/google-research/reasoning-bank.
reason: 该论文的核心研究内容（智能体记忆框架、经验学习与测试时缩放）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文未涉及数据筛选、过滤、去重、质量评估或推测解码、快速采样等技术。然而，论文提出的方法在智能体持续学习领域具有创新性，实验设计充分（涵盖网页浏览与软件工程基准），并开源了代码，因此作为独立研究具有一定价值，但与用户当前关注点相关性较低。
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型智能体越来越多地长期部署在实际场景中，它们自然会持续面对源源不断的新任务。然而，一个关键限制在于它们无法从积累的交互历史中有效学习，导致宝贵的经验被丢弃，并重复过去的错误。我们提出了 ReasoningBank，一种新颖的记忆框架，它能够从智能体自我判定为成功或失败的经验中，提炼出可泛化的推理策略。在测试阶段，智能体从 ReasoningBank 中检索相关记忆以指导其交互，并将新的学习成果整合回记忆库，从而实现随时间推移能力的持续提升。基于这一强大的经验学习器，我们进一步引入了记忆感知测试时扩展（MaTTS），它通过扩大智能体的交互经验规模来加速并多样化这一学习过程。通过为每个任务分配更多计算资源，智能体能生成大量、多样的经验，为合成更高质量的记忆提供丰富的对比信号。而更优质的记忆反过来又能指导更有效的扩展，从而在记忆与测试时扩展之间建立起强大的协同效应。在网页浏览和软件工程基准测试中，ReasoningBank  consistently 优于那些仅存储原始轨迹或只保留成功任务流程的现有记忆机制，在提升效果的同时也提高了效率；MaTTS 进一步放大了这些收益。这些发现确立了以记忆为驱动的经验扩展作为一个新的扩展维度，使智能体能够实现自我进化，并自然涌现出新的能力。我们的代码已公开于 https://github.com/google-research/reasoning-bank。

title: When Silence Matters: The Impact of Irrelevant Audio on Text Reasoning in Large Audio-Language Models
authors: Chen-An Li, Tzu-Han Lin, Hung-yi Lee
abstract: Large audio-language models (LALMs) unify speech and text processing, but their robustness in noisy real-world settings remains underexplored. We investigate how irrelevant audio, such as silence, synthetic noise, and environmental sounds, affects text reasoning tasks where audio is unnecessary. Across three text-based benchmarks, we find that even non-informative audio reduces accuracy and increases prediction volatility; the severity of interference scales with longer durations, higher amplitudes, and elevated decoding temperatures. Silence, often assumed neutral, destabilizes outputs as strongly as synthetic noise. While larger models show greater resilience, vulnerabilities persist across all evaluated systems. We further test mitigation strategies and find that prompting shows limited effectiveness, whereas self-consistency improves stability at the cost of increased computation. Our results reveal cross-modal interference as a key robustness challenge and highlight the need for efficient fusion strategies that preserve reasoning performance in the presence of irrelevant inputs.
reason: 该论文的研究主题（大型音频-语言模型在无关音频干扰下的文本推理鲁棒性）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文关注的是多模态模型在推理阶段的跨模态干扰问题，而非数据 curation/filtering 或解码速度优化。尽管论文本身设计严谨、结论清晰，对音频-语言模型领域有明确贡献，但对用户当前的研究焦点价值有限。
abstract_zh: 大型音频语言模型（LALMs）统一了语音与文本处理，但其在嘈杂真实环境中的鲁棒性尚未得到充分探索。本研究探讨了无关音频（如静音、合成噪声及环境声音）对无需音频输入的文本推理任务的影响。在三个基于文本的基准测试中，我们发现即使是非信息性音频也会降低准确率并增加预测波动性；干扰的严重程度随音频时长增加、振幅升高以及解码温度上升而加剧。通常被认为中性的静音，其使输出不稳定的程度与合成噪声相当。尽管更大规模的模型展现出更强的韧性，但所有评估系统均存在脆弱性。 further测试了缓解策略，发现提示工程效果有限，而自一致性方法虽能提升稳定性，却以增加计算成本为代价。我们的研究揭示了跨模态干扰作为关键鲁棒性挑战，并凸显了开发高效融合策略的必要性，以确保模型在存在无关输入时仍能保持推理性能。

title: Token-Level LLM Collaboration via FusionRoute
authors: Nuoya Xiong, Yuhang Zhou, Hanqing Zeng, Zhaorun Chen, Furong Huang, Shuchao Bi, Lizhu Zhang, Zhuokai Zhao
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strengths across diverse domains. However, achieving strong performance across these domains with a single general-purpose model typically requires scaling to sizes that are prohibitively expensive to train and deploy. On the other hand, while smaller domain-specialized models are much more efficient, they struggle to generalize beyond their training distributions. To address this dilemma, we propose FusionRoute, a robust and effective token-level multi-LLM collaboration framework in which a lightweight router simultaneously (i) selects the most suitable expert at each decoding step and (ii) contributes a complementary logit that refines or corrects the selected expert's next-token distribution via logit addition. Unlike existing token-level collaboration methods that rely solely on fixed expert outputs, we provide a theoretical analysis showing that pure expert-only routing is fundamentally limited: unless strong global coverage assumptions hold, it cannot in general realize the optimal decoding policy. By augmenting expert selection with a trainable complementary generator, FusionRoute expands the effective policy class and enables recovery of optimal value functions under mild conditions. Empirically, across both Llama-3 and Gemma-2 families and diverse benchmarks spanning mathematical reasoning, code generation, and instruction following, FusionRoute outperforms both sequence- and token-level collaboration, model merging, and direct fine-tuning, while remaining competitive with domain experts on their respective tasks.
reason: 该论文提出了一种token级的多LLM协作框架（FusionRoute），核心在于动态路由选择专家模型并融合logits以提升性能。虽然其token级路由机制与'推测性解码'领域在技术层面（如动态token分配）有表面相似性，但研究目标不同：推测性解码主要关注**解码加速**，而本文聚焦于**性能提升与模型协作**，未涉及加速或两遍解码的核心问题。同时，论文与'LLM训练中的数据选择'方向无直接关联。因此，与用户指定研究方向的相关性为中等，论文质量较好但非核心相关，适合快速浏览摘要以了解跨模型协作的新思路。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）在各领域均展现出优势，但构建单一通用高性能模型通常需要规模达到成本极其高昂的程度。相比之下，更高效的小型领域专家模型则难以在其训练分布之外泛化。为应对此困境，本文提出FusionRoute，一种稳健且有效的token级多LLM协作框架。该框架中，一个轻量级路由器在每一步解码时同时（i）选择最合适的专家，并（ii）生成一个互补的logit，通过logit相加来修正或纠正所选专家的下一个token分布。与仅依赖固定专家输出的现有token级协作方法不同，我们的理论分析表明，纯专家路由（expert-only routing）存在根本性局限：除非强全局覆盖假设成立，否则它通常无法实现最优解码策略。通过为专家选择配备一个可训练的互补生成器，FusionRoute扩展了有效的策略类别，并能在温和条件下恢复最优价值函数。实证上，在涵盖数学推理、代码生成和指令遵循的多样化基准测试中，无论针对Llama-3还是Gemma-2系列模型，FusionRoute均优于序列级和token级协作、模型合并以及直接微调方法，同时在各自任务上仍能保持与领域专家相当的性能。
core_contribution: 针对单一大模型效率与泛化难以兼顾的问题，本文提出FusionRoute框架，通过可训练路由器在token级动态选择专家并生成互补logit进行修正，理论上突破了纯专家路由的局限，实验证明其在多领域任务上优于现有协作与微调方法。

title: Dynamics Within Latent Chain-of-Thought: An Empirical Study of Causal Structure
authors: Zirui Li, Xuefeng Bai, Kehai Chen, Yizhi Li, Jian Yang, Chenghua Lin, Min Zhang
abstract: Latent or continuous chain-of-thought methods replace explicit textual rationales with a number of internal latent steps, but these intermediate computations are difficult to evaluate beyond correlation-based probes. In this paper, we view latent chain-of-thought as a manipulable causal process in representation space by modeling latent steps as variables in a structural causal model (SCM) and analyzing their effects through step-wise $\mathrm{do}$-interventions. We study two representative paradigms (i.e., Coconut and CODI) on both mathematical and general reasoning tasks to investigate three key questions: (1) which steps are causally necessary for correctness and when answers become decidable early; (2) how does influence propagate across steps, and how does this structure compare to explicit CoT; and (3) do intermediate trajectories retain competing answer modes, and how does output-level commitment differ from representational commitment across steps. We find that latent-step budgets behave less like homogeneous extra depth and more like staged functionality with non-local routing, and we identify a persistent gap between early output bias and late representational commitment. These results motivate mode-conditional and stability-aware analyses -- and corresponding training/decoding objectives -- as more reliable tools for interpreting and improving latent reasoning systems. Code is available at https://github.com/J1mL1/causal-latent-cot.
reason: 该论文研究latent chain-of-thought的因果结构与内部动态，属于模型可解释性与推理机制分析。用户研究方向聚焦于训练数据选择（llm-data-selection）与 speculative decoding 两类具体技术，二者与本文主题无直接关联。论文对理解推理过程有独立价值，但未涉及数据 curation/filtering 或快速解码策略，因此相关性低但有一定参考意义。
abstract_zh: 潜思维链方法用一系列内部潜步骤取代了显式的文本推理过程，但这些中间计算难以通过相关性探测之外的方法进行评估。本文通过将潜步骤建模为结构因果模型（SCM）中的变量，并利用逐步do-干预分析其效应，将潜思维链视为表示空间中可操作的因果过程。我们选取两种代表性范式（即Coconut与CODI），在数学推理与通用推理任务上研究三个关键问题：(1) 哪些步骤对答案正确性具有因果必要性，以及答案在何时早期可判定；(2) 影响如何跨步骤传播，其结构与显式思维链（CoT）相比有何异同；(3) 中间轨迹是否保留竞争性答案模式，输出层承诺与跨步骤表示层承诺存在何种差异。研究发现，潜步骤预算更倾向于呈现分阶段功能与非局部路由的特性，而非同质的额外深度；同时发现早期输出偏差与后期表示承诺之间存在持续落差。这些结果启示我们，模式条件性与稳定性感知分析——以及相应的训练/解码目标——是解读与改进潜推理系统更可靠的工具。代码已公开于 https://github.com/J1mL1/causal-latent-cot。

title: SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration
authors: Jialong Chen, Xander Xu, Hu Wei, Chuan Chen, Bing Zhao
abstract: Large language model (LLM)-powered agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in automating software engineering tasks such as static bug fixing, as evidenced by benchmarks like SWE-bench. However, in the real world, the development of mature software is typically predicated on complex requirement changes and long-term feature iterations -- a process that static, one-shot repair paradigms fail to capture. To bridge this gap, we propose \textbf{SWE-CI}, the first repository-level benchmark built upon the Continuous Integration loop, aiming to shift the evaluation paradigm for code generation from static, short-term \textit{functional correctness} toward dynamic, long-term \textit{maintainability}. The benchmark comprises 100 tasks, each corresponding on average to an evolution history spanning 233 days and 71 consecutive commits in a real-world code repository. SWE-CI requires agents to systematically resolve these tasks through dozens of rounds of analysis and coding iterations. SWE-CI provides valuable insights into how well agents can sustain code quality throughout long-term evolution.
reason: 该论文聚焦于LLM代理在软件工程长期代码维护任务中的评估，提出了SWE-CI基准测试，强调动态可维护性而非静态功能正确性。论文质量较高，设计合理，基于真实代码库历史数据。然而，与用户指定的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择或推测解码技术）无直接关联：未涉及数据 curation、filtering、curriculum learning 等数据选择关键词，也未提及 speculative decoding、fast decoding 等推理加速技术。因此，相关性低，但作为AI for software engineering领域的基准测试，具有一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体在自动化软件工程任务（如静态缺陷修复）中已展现出强大能力，这由SWE-bench等基准测试所证实。然而，在现实世界中，成熟软件的开发通常以复杂的需求变更和长期的功能迭代为前提——而静态的、一次性修复范式无法捕捉这一过程。为弥合这一差距，我们提出了\textbf{SWE-CI}，这是首个基于持续集成循环构建的仓库级基准测试，旨在将代码生成的评估范式从静态的、短期的“功能正确性”转向动态的、长期的“可维护性”。该基准测试包含100个任务，每个任务平均对应真实代码库中历时233天、包含71个连续提交的演化历史。SWE-CI要求智能体通过数十轮分析与编码迭代来系统性地解决这些任务。它为评估智能体在长期演化过程中维持代码质量的能力提供了宝贵的洞察。

title: Test-Time Adaptation via Many-Shot Prompting: Benefits, Limits, and Pitfalls
authors: Shubhangi Upasani, Chen Wu, Jay Rainton, Bo Li, Urmish Thakker, Changran Hu, Qizheng Zhang
abstract: Test-time adaptation enables large language models (LLMs) to modify their behavior at inference without updating model parameters. A common approach is many-shot prompting, where large numbers of in-context learning (ICL) examples are injected as an input-space test-time update. Although performance can improve as more demonstrations are added, the reliability and limits of this update mechanism remain poorly understood, particularly for open-source models. We present an empirical study of many-shot prompting across tasks and model backbones, analyzing how performance varies with update magnitude, example ordering, and selection policy. We further study Dynamic and Reinforced ICL as alternative test-time update strategies that control which information is injected and how it constrains model behavior. We find that many-shot prompting is effective for structured tasks where demonstrations provide high information gain, but is highly sensitive to selection strategy and often shows limited benefits for open-ended generation tasks. Overall, we characterize the practical limits of prompt-based test-time adaptation and outline when input-space updates are beneficial versus harmful.
reason: 该论文研究many-shot prompting作为测试时适应（test-time adaptation）的方法，其核心是分析推理阶段通过输入大量示例来调整模型行为的效果与限制。虽然文中涉及‘示例选择策略’（selection policy），这与您研究方向中的‘数据选择’（data selection）在概念上有间接关联（都涉及‘选择’），但本质不同：您的研究聚焦于**训练前/训练中**的数据集构建、筛选与优化（如数据质量、去重、课程学习），而本文聚焦于**推理时**的提示工程（in-context learning示例的选取与组织）。论文未涉及推测解码（speculative decoding）相关技术。因此，论文与您的研究方向存在中等程度的交叉（主要关联‘数据选择’的广义概念），但非直接核心。论文本身为规范的实证研究，分析较全面，但主题不属于您提供的两个具体领域标签范畴。
abstract_zh: 测试时适应使大语言模型能够在推理时不更新模型参数即可修改其行为。一种常见方法是大量样本提示，即将大量上下文学习示例作为输入注入以实现测试时更新。尽管添加更多演示可能提升性能，但该更新机制的可靠性与限制，尤其是对开源模型，仍缺乏充分理解。我们呈现了一项跨任务与模型骨干的实证研究，分析了性能如何随更新幅度、示例排序和选择策略变化。进一步研究了动态与强化上下文学习作为替代的测试时更新策略，以控制注入的信息及其如何约束模型行为。我们发现，大量样本提示对示例提供高信息增益的结构化任务有效，但高度依赖选择策略，且在开放生成任务中常显示有限收益。总体而言，我们刻画了基于提示的测试时适应的实际限制，并概述了输入空间更新何时有益、何时有害。
core_contribution: 该论文系统研究了测试时适应中大量样本提示方法的效益边界与敏感性，并提出了动态及强化上下文学习作为更可控的替代更新策略。

title: VorTEX: Various overlap ratio for Target speech EXtraction
authors: Ro-hoon Oh, Jihwan Seol, Bugeun Kim
abstract: Target speech extraction (TSE) aims to recover a target speaker's voice from a mixture. While recent text-prompted approaches have shown promise, most approaches assume fully overlapped mixtures, limiting insight into behavior across realistic overlap ratios. We introduce VorTEX (Various overlap ratio for Target speech EXtraction), a text-prompted TSE architecture with a Decoupled Adaptive Multi-branch (DAM) Fusion block that separates primary extraction from auxiliary regularization pathways. To enable controlled analysis, we construct PORTE, a two-speaker dataset spanning overlap ratios from 0% to 100%. We further propose Suppression Ratio on Energy (SuRE), a diagnostic metric that detects suppression behavior not captured by conventional measures. Experiments show that existing models exhibit suppression or residual interference under overlap, whereas VorTEX achieves the highest separation fidelity across 20-100% overlap (e.g., 5.50 dB at 20% and 2.04 dB at 100%) while maintaining zero SuRE, indicating robust extraction without suppression-driven artifacts.
reason: 该论文研究目标语音提取（Target Speech Extraction, TSE），属于语音信号处理/语音分离领域。其核心工作包括设计用于处理不同语音重叠比例的神经网络架构（DAM Fusion）、构建特定数据集（PORTE）以及提出评估指标（SuRE）。这些内容与用户研究方向中的'LLM training: data selection'（关注LLM训练中的数据选择与处理）和'speculative decoding'（关注LLM推理加速技术）在核心问题、技术方法和应用场景上均无直接关联。论文未涉及大语言模型（LLM）训练、数据策展、推测解码或任何相关概念，因此与用户研究方向无价值关联。

title: To See is Not to Master: Teaching LLMs to Use Private Libraries for Code Generation
authors: Yitong Zhang, Chengze Li, Ruize Chen, Guowei Yang, Xiaoran Jia, Yijie Ren, Jia Li
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown strong potential for code generation, yet they remain limited in private-library-oriented code generation, where the goal is to generate code using APIs from private libraries. Existing approaches mainly rely on retrieving private-library API documentation and injecting relevant knowledge into the context at inference time. However, our study shows that this is insufficient: even given accurate required knowledge, LLMs still struggle to invoke private-library APIs effectively.
  To address this limitation, we propose PriCoder, an approach that teaches LLMs to invoke private-library APIs through automatically synthesized data. Specifically, PriCoder models private-library data synthesis as the construction of a graph, and alternates between two graph operators: (1) Progressive Graph Evolution, which improves data diversity by progressively synthesizing more diverse training samples from basic ones, and (2) Multidimensional Graph Pruning, which improves data quality through a rigorous filtering pipeline. To support rigorous evaluation, we construct two new benchmarks based on recently released libraries that are unfamiliar to the tested models. Experiments on three mainstream LLMs show that PriCoder substantially improves private-library-oriented code generation, yielding gains of over 20% in pass@1 in many settings, while causing negligible impact on general code generation capability. Our code and benchmarks are publicly available at https://github.com/eniacode/PriCoder.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是提出PriCoder方法，通过自动合成数据（基于图构建与操作）来提升LLM在私有库代码生成任务上的性能。其关键技术‘Progressive Graph Evolution’和‘Multidimensional Graph Pruning’直接对应研究方向‘llm-data-selection’中的数据合成、数据多样性增强、数据质量控制与过滤（pruning）等核心概念。论文针对数据质量与多样性问题提出了新颖且有效的解决方案，实验设计严谨，结果显著，并开源代码与基准，属于高质量、高度相关的研究，值得精读与分析。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成方面展现出巨大潜力，但在面向私有库的代码生成任务中仍存在局限，该任务的目标是使用私有库的API生成代码。现有方法主要依赖检索私有库API文档并在推理时注入相关知识。然而，我们的研究表明这是不够的：即使提供了准确所需的知识，LLMs在有效调用私有库API方面仍然吃力。为应对此限制，我们提出了PriCoder，一种通过自动合成数据来教导LLMs调用私有库API的方法。具体而言，PriCoder将私有库数据合成建模为图的构建，并在两个图算子之间交替进行：(1) 渐进式图演变，通过从基础样本逐步合成更多样化的训练样本来提升数据多样性；(2) 多维图剪枝，通过严格的过滤流程提升数据质量。为支持严谨评估，我们基于近期发布的、对测试模型而言陌生的库构建了两个新基准。在三个主流LLMs上的实验表明，PriCoder显著提升了面向私有库的代码生成能力，在许多场景下pass@1提升超过20%，同时对通用代码生成能力的影响微乎其微。我们的代码和基准已公开。
method_highlights: 核心创新：将私有库代码数据合成问题抽象为图构建与操作问题，而非简单的文本模板填充或检索增强。
渐进式图演变：从一个简单的‘种子’代码图（如单一API调用）开始，通过系统性地应用预定义的图变换操作（如添加相邻API调用、引入条件分支、循环结构）来逐步演化出复杂多样的代码样本。其巧妙之处在于以结构化的方式保证生成代码的语法正确性和逻辑连贯性，同时探索广阔的代码模式空间。
多维图剪枝：设计了一个多阶段的严格过滤管道，基于静态分析（如类型匹配、控制流有效性）、动态执行（如单元测试通过）以及语义相似度等多维度指标对生成的图（代码）进行筛选和去重。其关键在于结合静态与动态证据，确保最终训练数据的高质量与高实用性，而非仅依赖表面相似度。
数据驱动教学：最终使用此自动构建的高质量、高多样性数据集对LLM进行微调，使其‘学会’而非‘检索到’私有库API的典型使用模式，从根本上提升其调用能力。
experiment_highlights: 性能提升显著：在三个主流LLMs上，PriCoder在面向私有库的代码生成任务（pass@1）上实现了超过20%的绝对提升，验证了方法的有效性。
通用能力无损：实验表明，经PriCoder微调后的模型在通用代码生成基准（如HumanEval）上的性能与原始模型相比几乎没有下降，证明了方法的针对性和稳定性。
新基准构建：论文基于近期发布但模型未见的真实库（如`pylatex`, `pyhdfe`）构建了两个新的、更具挑战性的评估基准，避免了现有基准可能存在的训练数据污染问题，使评估更严谨。
消融研究有力：通过消融实验证明了‘渐进式图演变’对数据多样性和‘多维图剪枝’对数据质量的关键贡献，二者结合方能达到最佳效果。
对比基线充分：与强大的检索增强生成基线（如使用完美Oracle检索）相比，PriCoder仍能取得显著优势，证明了单纯提供知识不足以解决问题，需要模型内化‘使用技能’。
pros: 问题定位精准：深刻指出私有库代码生成的核心瓶颈是模型缺乏‘API invocation skill’（API调用技能），而非单纯的知识缺失，超越了传统RAG思路。
方法系统性强：提出的图构建、演变、剪枝框架提供了一个从数据生成到筛选的完整、自动化解决方案，减少了对手工编写测试用例或示例的依赖。
性能突破明显：在未见过的私有库上取得20%+的pass@1提升，且不损害通用能力，实用价值高。
可复现性高：公开了完整的代码、合成工具链以及新评估基准，为社区提供了宝贵的资源和清晰的复现路径。
cons: 合成数据偏差风险：自动合成的代码可能无法覆盖私有库所有真实、复杂或边缘的使用场景，模型可能学到的是‘典型模式’而非‘全部模式’，在遇到非常规用法时可能失效。
库的依赖性：方法效能高度依赖于对目标私有库的API签名、类型信息及基本用法的了解（用于构建种子图和定义图变换规则）。对于文档极差或高度动态的库，应用难度大。
计算成本较高：图演变和多重剪枝（尤其是涉及动态执行测试）的过程在数据合成阶段计算开销较大，可能限制其扩展到超大规模库或极多API的场景。
评估范围有限：实验仅涉及Python库和代码生成任务，未探讨该方法在其他编程语言（如Java、C++）或更广泛的任务（如代码补全、重构）上的泛化能力。
模型规模效应未充分探讨：实验在三个特定尺寸的LLMs上进行，未系统分析PriCoder对不同参数规模模型（如极小模型vs.超大模型）的影响是否存在差异。
reproducibility: 可复现性高。论文公开了完整的源代码（包括数据合成工具、训练/评估脚本）、新构建的基准数据集以及详细的实验配置（模型版本、微调超参数、评估设置）。方法描述清晰，图算子定义具体，为他人重复实验提供了充分条件。
task_list: 仔细阅读论文附录，理解图演变操作（Graph Evolution Operators）和剪枝规则（Pruning Rules）的具体设计细节与实现。
运行论文提供的代码，在给定的小规模示例库上复现数据合成流程，直观理解从种子图到最终训练样本的生成与过滤过程。
使用PriCoder在另一个未在论文中出现的、较小的私有库（如一个内部工具库）上进行端到端实验，验证其迁移能力。
分析合成数据集与模型错误案例，探究模型失败的具体模式（是类型错误、逻辑错误还是API误用？），以指导未来改进。
尝试将PriCoder的图合成思想简化后，应用于开源代码库的代码补全数据生成，评估其通用性。
研究PriCoder合成数据与人工编写示例在分布上的差异，量化‘多样性’和‘质量’的具体指标。

title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing: Balancing Profitability,Stability and Fairness
authors: Krishna Kumar Neelakanta Pillai Santha Kumari Amma
abstract: Dynamic pricing in competitive retail markets requires strategies that adapt to fluctuating demand and competitor behavior. In this work, we present a systematic empirical evaluation of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) approaches-specifically MAPPO and MADDPG-for dynamic price optimization under competition. Using a simulated marketplace environment derived from real-world retail data, we benchmark these algorithms against an Independent DDPG (IDDPG) baseline, a widely used independent learner in MARL literature. We evaluate profit performance, stability across random seeds, fairness, and training efficiency. Our results show that MAPPO consistently achieves the highest average returns with low variance, offering a stable and reproducible approach for competitive price optimization, while MADDPG achieves slightly lower profit but the fairest profit distribution among agents. These findings demonstrate that MARL methods-particularly MAPPO-provide a scalable and stable alternative to independent learning approaches for dynamic retail pricing.
reason: 该论文研究多智能体强化学习（MARL）在动态定价中的应用，核心内容为算法（MAPPO, MADDPG）比较与商业场景优化。用户的研究方向聚焦于大语言模型（LLM）训练中的'数据选择'与'推测解码'两大技术领域。两者在研究对象（商业定价 vs. LLM训练）、核心技术（MARL vs. 数据工程/解码算法）上均无直接关联。尽管论文本身方法论严谨、实验设计完整，属于质量较好的MARL应用研究，但因其主题与用户指定方向完全无关，对用户的研究工作无直接参考价值，故评为1分（相关性很低，价值有限）。

title: What on Earth is AlphaEarth? Hierarchical structure and functional interpretability for global land cover
authors: Ivan Felipe Benavides-Martinez, Justin Guthrie, Jhon Edwin Arias, Yeison Alberto Garces-Gomez, Angela Ines Guzman-Alvis, Cristiam Victoriano Portilla-Cabrera, Somnath Mondal, Andrew J. Allyn, Auroop R. Ganguly
abstract: Geospatial foundation models generate high-dimensional embeddings that achieve strong predictive performance, yet their internal organization remains obscure, limiting their scientific use. Recent interpretability studies relate Google AlphaEarth Foundations (GAEF) embeddings to continuous environmental variables, but it is still unclear whether the embedding space exhibits a functional or hierarchical organization, in which some dimensions act as specialized representations while others encode shared or broader geospatial structure. In this work, we propose a functional interpretability framework that reverse-engineers the role of embedding dimensions by characterizing their contribution to land cover structure from observed classification behavior. The approach combines large-scale experimentation with a structural analysis of embedding-class relationships based on feature importance patterns and progressive ablation. Our results show that embedding dimensions exhibit consistent and non-uniform functional behavior, allowing them to be categorized along a hierarchical functional spectrum: specialist dimensions associated with specific land cover classes, low- and mid-generalist dimensions capturing shared characteristics between classes, and highgeneralist dimensions reflecting broader environmental gradients. Critically, we find that accurate land cover classification (98% of baseline performance) can be achieved using as few as 2 to 12 of the 64 available dimensions, depending on the class. This demonstrates substantial redundancy in the embedding space and offers a pathway toward significant reductions in computational cost. Together, these findings reveal that AlphaEarth embeddings are not only physically informative, but also functionally organized into a hierarchical structure, providing practical guidance for dimension selection in operational classification tasks.
reason: 该论文的研究内容（地理空间基础模型AlphaEarth的嵌入空间功能可解释性与维度选择）与用户指定的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在核心问题、应用场景和技术目标上均无直接关联。论文虽在自身领域（地理空间AI）具有方法严谨性和发现价值，但完全不涉及LLM、解码加速或数据策展等关键词，因此对用户研究工作的参考价值极低。

title: MHPO: Modulated Hazard-aware Policy Optimization for Stable Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hongjun Wang, Wei Liu, Weibo Gu, Xing Sun, Kai Han
abstract: Regulating the importance ratio is critical for the training stability of Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) based frameworks. However, prevailing ratio control methods, such as hard clipping, suffer from non-differentiable boundaries and vanishing gradient regions, failing to maintain gradient fidelity. Furthermore, these methods lack a hazard-aware mechanism to adaptively suppress extreme deviations, leaving the optimization process vulnerable to abrupt policy shifts. To address these challenges, we propose Modulated Hazard-aware Policy Optimization (MHPO), a novel framework designed for robust and stable reinforcement learning. The proposed MHPO introduces a Log-Fidelity Modulator (LFM) to map unbounded importance ratios into a bounded, differentiable domain. This mechanism effectively prevents high-variance outlier tokens from destabilizing the loss landscape while ensuring global gradient stability. Complementarily, a Decoupled Hazard Penalty (DHP) integrates cumulative hazard functions from survival analysis to independently regulate positive and negative policy shifts. By shaping the optimization landscape with hazard-aware penalties, the proposed MHPO achieves fine-grained regulation of asymmetric policy shifts simultaneously mitigating mode collapse from over-expansion and preventing policy erosion from catastrophic contraction within a stabilized trust region. Extensive evaluations on diverse reasoning benchmarks across both text-based and vision-language tasks demonstrate that MHPO consistently outperforms existing methods, achieving superior performance while significantly enhancing training stability.
reason: 该论文的核心研究内容是强化学习（特别是GRPO框架）中的策略优化与稳定性控制，提出了MHPO框架及其LFM、DHP组件。这与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在技术主题、核心问题和应用场景上均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据筛选、质量评估、缓存/草稿机制、解码加速等关键词。尽管强化学习技术可能间接应用于LLM训练（如RLHF阶段），但本文的贡献点完全属于通用强化学习算法改进，与用户指定的具体技术领域不匹配，因此相关性极低。

title: Transformers Can Learn Rules They've Never Seen: Proof of Computation Beyond Interpolation
authors: Andy Gray
abstract: A central question in the LLM debate is whether transformers can infer rules absent from training, or whether apparent generalisation reduces to similarity-based interpolation over observed examples. We test a strong interpolation-only hypothesis in two controlled settings: one where interpolation is ruled out by construction and proof, and one where success requires emitting intermediate symbolic derivations rather than only final answers. In Experiment 1, we use a cellular automaton with a pure XOR transition rule and remove specific local input patterns from training; since XOR is linearly inseparable, each held-out pattern's nearest neighbours have the opposite label, so similarity-based predictors fail on the held-out region. Yet a two-layer transformer recovers the rule (best 100%; 47/60 converged runs), and circuit extraction identifies XOR computation. Performance depends on multi-step constraint propagation: without unrolling, accuracy matches output bias (63.1%), while soft unrolling reaches 96.7%. In Experiment 2, we study symbolic operator chains over integers with one operator pair held out; the model must emit intermediate steps and a final answer in a proof-like format. Across all 49 holdout pairs, the transformer exceeds every interpolation baseline (mean 41.8%, up to 78.6%; mean KRR 4.3%; KNN and MLP score 0% on every pair), while removing intermediate-step supervision degrades performance. Together with a construction showing that a standard transformer block can implement exact local Boolean rules, these results provide an existence proof that transformers can learn rule structure not directly observed in training and express it explicitly, ruling out the strongest architectural form of interpolation-only accounts: that transformers cannot in principle discover and communicate unseen rules, while leaving open when such behaviour arises in large-scale language training.
reason: 该论文的研究核心是探究Transformer模型进行规则泛化与发现的能力（是否能在未见数据上学习并表达新规则），与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在主题上无直接关联。论文的实验设计（细胞自动机XOR规则、符号算子链）和讨论重点（插值假设、规则提取、中间步骤监督）均未涉及数据筛选、数据质量、推测采样或加速解码等关键词。尽管论文本身质量较高，实验严谨，对理解Transformer的泛化机制有重要贡献，但与用户指定的研究领域相关性很低。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型辩论中的一个核心问题是：Transformer能否推断出训练数据中未包含的规则，还是说其表面的泛化能力实际上只是对已观察示例进行基于相似性的插值？我们在两个受控场景中检验了强插值-only假设：一个通过构造和证明排除了插值的可能性，另一个要求模型输出中间符号推导而不仅仅是最终答案。实验1中，我们使用具有纯异或转移规则的元胞自动机，并从训练中移除特定的局部输入模式；由于异或在特征空间线性不可分，每个被保留模式的最邻近邻居都具有相反的标签，因此基于相似性的预测器在保留区域失效。然而，一个双层Transformer成功恢复了该规则（最佳准确率100%；47/60次运行收敛），且电路提取识别出了异或计算过程。性能依赖于多步约束传播：若不展开，准确率仅与输出偏置相当（63.1%），而软展开可达96.7%。实验2中，我们研究整数上的符号算子链，其中一对算子被保留在训练集之外；模型必须以类似证明的格式输出中间步骤和最终答案。在所有49组被保留的算子对中，Transformer的表现均超过所有插值基线（平均41.8%，最高78.6%；平均核岭回归4.3%；K近邻与多层感知机在每对上均为0%），且移除中间步骤监督会降低性能。结合一个展示标准Transformer块可实现精确局部布尔规则的构造，这些结果提供了存在性证明：Transformer能够学习训练中未直接观察到的规则结构并明确表达，从而排除了最强的架构形式上的纯插值解释——即Transformer原则上无法发现和传递未见规则；同时，也为此类行为在大型语言模型训练中何时出现留下了开放问题。

title: SCE-LITE-HQ: Smooth visual counterfactual explanations with generative foundation models
authors: Ahmed Zeid, Sidney Bender
abstract: Modern neural networks achieve strong performance but remain difficult to interpret in high-dimensional visual domains. Counterfactual explanations (CFEs) provide a principled approach to interpreting black-box predictions by identifying minimal input changes that alter model outputs. However, existing CFE methods often rely on dataset-specific generative models and incur substantial computational cost, limiting their scalability to high-resolution data. We propose SCE-LITE-HQ, a scalable framework for counterfactual generation that leverages pretrained generative foundation models without task-specific retraining. The method operates in the latent space of the generator, incorporates smoothed gradients to improve optimization stability, and applies mask-based diversification to promote realistic and structurally diverse counterfactuals. We evaluate SCE-LITE-HQ on natural and medical datasets using a desiderata-driven evaluation protocol. Results show that SCE-LITE-HQ produces valid, realistic, and diverse counterfactuals competitive with or outperforming existing baselines, while avoiding the overhead of training dedicated generative models.
reason: 该论文的研究内容（基于生成基础模型的视觉反事实解释）与用户提供的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在核心目标、技术场景和应用领域上均无直接关联。论文关注计算机视觉领域的模型可解释性，而用户方向聚焦于大语言模型的高效训练与推理优化。尽管论文使用了‘生成基础模型’这一术语，但其指代的通常是图像/视觉生成模型（如Stable Diffusion），与LLM的文本生成及优化技术路径不同。因此，对用户的研究工作价值非常有限。

title: Early Quantization Shrinks Codebook: A Simple Fix for Diversity-Preserving Tokenization
authors: Wenhao Zhao, Qiran Zou, Rushi Shah, Yudi Wu, Zhouhan Lin, Dianbo Liu
abstract: Vector quantization is a technique in machine learning that discretizes continuous representations into a set of discrete vectors. It is widely employed in tokenizing data representations for large language models, diffusion models, and other generative models. Despite its prevalence, the characteristics and behaviors of vector quantization in generative models remain largely underexplored. In this study, we systematically investigate the issue of collapses in vector quantization, where collapsed representations are observed across discrete codebook tokens and continuous latent embeddings. By leveraging both synthetic and real datasets, we identify the severity of each type of collapses and triggering conditions. Our analysis reveals that random initialization and limited encoder capacity result in tokens collapse and embeddings collapse. Building on these findings, we propose potential solutions aimed at mitigating each collapse. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first comprehensive study examining representation collapsing problems in vector quantization.
reason: 该论文的核心研究方向是向量量化（Vector Quantization）中的表示坍缩问题及其修复方法，主要关注模型内部表示学习与优化。这与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测性解码）无直接关联。虽然向量量化技术可能在某些LLM架构（如VQ-VAE）或分词方法中有所应用，但论文并未涉及数据筛选、课程学习、影响函数（llm-data-selection），也未涉及解码加速、推测采样等（speculative-decoding）主题。论文本身具有系统性分析和实证贡献，但针对用户的研究目标，相关性较低，仅作为广义的表示学习文献存在一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 向量量化是机器学习中的一种技术，它将连续表示离散化为一组离散向量。该方法广泛应用于大语言模型、扩散模型及其他生成模型的数据表示标记化过程中。尽管其应用普遍，但向量量化在生成模型中的特性和行为仍未得到充分探索。本研究系统性地 investigates 了向量量化中的坍缩问题，即在离散码本标记和连续潜在嵌入中观察到的表示坍缩现象。通过结合合成数据集与真实数据集，我们识别了每种坍缩类型的严重程度及其触发条件。分析表明，随机初始化与编码器容量有限分别导致了标记坍缩与嵌入坍缩。基于上述发现，我们提出了旨在缓解每种坍缩的潜在解决方案。据我们所知，这是首个针对向量量化中表示坍缩问题进行的全面研究。

title: SENSE: Efficient EEG-to-Text via Privacy-Preserving Semantic Retrieval
authors: Akshaj Murhekar, Christina Liu, Abhijit Mishra, Shounak Roychowdhury, Jacek Gwizdka
abstract: Decoding brain activity into natural language is a major challenge in AI with important applications in assistive communication, neurotechnology, and human-computer interaction. Most existing Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) approaches rely on memory-intensive fine-tuning of Large Language Models (LLMs) or encoder-decoder models on raw EEG signals, resulting in expensive training pipelines, limited accessibility, and potential exposure of sensitive neural data. We introduce SENSE (SEmantic Neural Sparse Extraction), a lightweight and privacy-preserving framework that translates non-invasive electroencephalography (EEG) into text without LLM fine-tuning. SENSE decouples decoding into two stages: on-device semantic retrieval and prompt-based language generation. EEG signals are locally mapped to a discrete textual space to extract a non-sensitive Bag-of-Words (BoW), which conditions an off-the-shelf LLM to synthesize fluent text in a zero-shot manner. The EEG-to-keyword module contains only ~6M parameters and runs fully on-device, ensuring raw neural signals remain local while only abstract semantic cues interact with language models. Evaluated on a 128-channel EEG dataset across six subjects, SENSE matches or surpasses the generative quality of fully fine-tuned baselines such as Thought2Text while substantially reducing computational overhead. By localizing neural decoding and sharing only derived textual cues, SENSE provides a scalable and privacy-aware retrieval-augmented architecture for next-generation BCIs.

title: REAL: Regression-Aware Reinforcement Learning for LLM-as-a-Judge
authors: Yasi Zhang, Tianyu Chen, Mingyuan Zhou, Oscar Leong, Ying Nian Wu, Michal Lukasik
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as automated evaluators that assign numeric scores to model outputs, a paradigm known as LLM-as-a-Judge. However, standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods typically rely on binary rewards (e.g., 0-1 accuracy), thereby ignoring the ordinal structure inherent in regression tasks; for instance, they fail to recognize that predicting 4 is significantly better than predicting 1 when the ground truth is 5. Conversely, existing regression-aware approaches are often confined to Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), limiting their ability to explore optimal reasoning paths. To bridge this gap, we propose \textbf{REAL} (\underline{RE}gression-\underline{A}ware Reinforcement \underline{L}earning), a principled RL framework designed to optimize regression rewards, and also proven to be optimal for correlation metrics. A key technical challenge is that the regression objective is explicitly policy-dependent, thus invalidating standard policy gradient methods. To address this, we employ the generalized policy gradient estimator, which naturally decomposes optimization into two complementary components: (1) exploration over Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trajectory, and (2) regression-aware prediction refinement of the final score. Extensive experiments across model scales (8B to 32B) demonstrate that REAL consistently outperforms both regression-aware SFT baselines and standard RL methods, exhibiting significantly better generalization on out-of-domain benchmarks. On Qwen3-32B specifically, we achieve gains of +8.40 Pearson and +7.20 Spearman correlation over the SFT baseline, and +18.30/+11.20 over the base model. These findings highlight the critical value of integrating regression objectives into RL exploration for accurate LLM evaluation.

title: Personalized Fall Detection by Balancing Data with Selective Feedback Using Contrastive Learning
authors: Awatif Yasmin, Tarek Mahmud, Sana Alamgeer, Anne H. H. Ngu
abstract: Personalized fall detection models can significantly improve accuracy by adapting to individual motion patterns, yet their effectiveness is often limited by the scarcity of real-world fall data and the dominance of non-fall feedback samples. This imbalance biases the model toward routine activities and weakens its sensitivity to true fall events. To address this challenge, we propose a personalization framework that combines semi-supervised clustering with contrastive learning to identify and balance the most informative user feedback samples. The framework is evaluated under three retraining strategies, including Training from Scratch (TFS), Transfer Learning (TL), and Few-Shot Learning (FSL), to assess adaptability across learning paradigms. Real-time experiments with ten participants show that the TFS approach achieves the highest performance, with up to a 25% improvement over the baseline, while FSL achieves the second-highest performance with a 7% improvement, demonstrating the effectiveness of selective personalization for real-world deployment.

title: Noise-Response Calibration: A Causal Intervention Protocol for LLM-Judges
authors: Maxim Khomiakov, Jes Frellsen
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as automated judges and synthetic labelers, especially in low-label settings. Yet these systems are stochastic and often overconfident, which makes deployment decisions difficult when external ground truth is limited. We propose a practical calibration protocol based on controlled input interventions: if noise severity increases, task performance should exhibit a statistically significant deterioration trend. We operationalize this with a slope-based hypothesis test over repeated trials, using signal-to-noise-ratio (SNR) perturbations for tabular data and lexical perturbations for text data. Across UCI tabular benchmarks and four text classification datasets, we find clear modality-dependent behavior. Our results reveal a modality gap: while text-based judges degrade predictably, the majority of tabular datasets show a lack of statistically significant performance deterioration even under significant signal-to-noise reduction. Interestingly we find that model performance is lower on datasets that are insensitive to noise interventions. We present a reproducible methodology and reporting protocol for robust LLM-judge calibration under distribution shift.
reason: 论文主题聚焦于LLM作为评判者的校准方法，使用因果干预和噪声扰动实验，与用户研究方向（LLM数据选择和speculative decoding）无直接关联。论文质量中等，有实验设计和可重复性，但核心内容不涉及数据选择、过滤或解码优化，因此相关性低，仅对LLM应用有一般参考价值。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）正日益被用作自动评判者和合成标签器，尤其是在标签稀缺的场景中。然而，这些系统具有随机性且往往过度自信，当外部真实标签有限时，这使得部署决策变得困难。我们提出了一种基于受控输入干预的实用校准协议：如果噪声严重程度增加，任务性能应表现出统计显著的退化趋势。我们通过在重复试验中采用基于斜率的假设检验来实现这一点，对表格数据使用信噪比（SNR）扰动，对文本数据使用词汇扰动。在UCI表格基准和四个文本分类数据集上，我们发现了清晰的模态依赖行为。我们的结果揭示了一个模态差距：虽然基于文本的评判者可预测地退化，但大多数表格数据集即使在显著的信噪比降低下，也缺乏统计显著的性能退化。有趣的是，我们发现模型在对噪声干预不敏感的数据集上性能较低。我们提出了一种可重复的方法和报告协议，用于在分布偏移下进行稳健的大语言模型评判者校准。

title: Self-Conditioned Denoising for Atomistic Representation Learning
authors: Tynan Perez, Rafael Gomez-Bombarelli
abstract: The success of large-scale pretraining in NLP and computer vision has catalyzed growing efforts to develop analogous foundation models for the physical sciences. However, pretraining strategies using atomistic data remain underexplored. To date, large-scale supervised pretraining on DFT force-energy labels has provided the strongest performance gains to downstream property prediction, out-performing existing methods of self-supervised learning (SSL) which remain limited to ground-state geometries, and/or single domains of atomistic data. We address these shortcomings with Self-Conditioned Denoising (SCD), a backbone-agnostic reconstruction objective that utilizes self-embeddings for conditional denoising across any domain of atomistic data, including small molecules, proteins, periodic materials, and 'non-equilibrium' geometries. When controlled for backbone architecture and pretraining dataset, SCD significantly outperforms previous SSL methods on downstream benchmarks and matches or exceeds the performance of supervised force-energy pretraining. We show that a small, fast GNN pretrained by SCD can achieve competitive or superior performance to larger models pretrained on significantly larger labeled or unlabeled datasets, across tasks in multiple domains. Our code is available at: https://github.com/TyJPerez/SelfConditionedDenoisingAtoms

title: Variational Rectification Inference for Learning with Noisy Labels
authors: Haoliang Sun, Qi Wei, Lei Feng, Yupeng Hu, Fan Liu, Hehe Fan, Yilong Yin
abstract: Label noise has been broadly observed in real-world datasets. To mitigate the negative impact of overfitting to label noise for deep models, effective strategies (\textit{e.g.}, re-weighting, or loss rectification) have been broadly applied in prevailing approaches, which have been generally learned under the meta-learning scenario. Despite the robustness of noise achieved by the probabilistic meta-learning models, they usually suffer from model collapse that degenerates generalization performance. In this paper, we propose variational rectification inference (VRI) to formulate the adaptive rectification for loss functions as an amortized variational inference problem and derive the evidence lower bound under the meta-learning framework. Specifically, VRI is constructed as a hierarchical Bayes by treating the rectifying vector as a latent variable, which can rectify the loss of the noisy sample with the extra randomness regularization and is, therefore, more robust to label noise. To achieve the inference of the rectifying vector, we approximate its conditional posterior with an amortization meta-network. By introducing the variational term in VRI, the conditional posterior is estimated accurately and avoids collapsing to a Dirac delta function, which can significantly improve the generalization performance. The elaborated meta-network and prior network adhere to the smoothness assumption, enabling the generation of reliable rectification vectors. Given a set of clean meta-data, VRI can be efficiently meta-learned within the bi-level optimization programming. Besides, theoretical analysis guarantees that the meta-network can be efficiently learned with our algorithm. Comprehensive comparison experiments and analyses validate its effectiveness for robust learning with noisy labels, particularly in the presence of open-set noise.
reason: 该论文研究的是带有噪声标签的鲁棒学习问题，核心贡献是提出一种基于变分推理的损失函数修正方法。虽然其目标（提升数据/训练质量）与您研究方向中的‘LLM training: data selection’在宏观目标上存在间接关联（都涉及提升数据或训练过程的可靠性），但论文的具体技术路径（变分贝叶斯推理、噪声标签的损失修正）与您列出的关键词（如数据选择、数据筛选、推测解码、快速解码）无直接重叠。论文未涉及LLM、推测解码或任何与解码速度/效率相关的内容，也未讨论数据 curation、filtering 或 pruning 等具体数据选择技术。因此，与您的研究方向相关性较低，但其作为机器学习领域处理噪声问题的研究，具有一定的参考价值。
abstract_zh: 标签噪声在现实世界数据集中普遍存在。为了缓解深度模型对标签噪声过拟合的负面影响，现有主流方法通常采用元学习框架下的有效策略（如重加权或损失校正）进行训练。尽管基于概率元学习模型能获得一定的噪声鲁棒性，但它们常因模型崩溃而损害泛化性能。本文提出变分校正推断（VRI），将自适应损失校正的构建问题形式化为一个分摊变分推断问题，并在元学习框架下推导出证据下界。具体而言，VRI通过将校正向量视为潜变量来构建层次贝叶斯模型，该向量能利用额外的随机性正则化对噪声样本的损失进行校正，因而对标签噪声更具鲁棒性。为推断校正向量，我们采用一个分摊元网络来近似其后验条件分布。通过在VRI中引入变分项，条件后验得以精确估计并避免退化为狄拉克δ函数，从而显著提升泛化性能。精心设计的元网络与先验网络遵循平滑性假设，能够生成可靠的校正向量。给定一组干净的元数据，VRI可在双层优化框架下高效进行元学习。此外，理论分析保证了我们的算法能高效学习元网络。全面的对比实验与分析验证了VRI在带噪声标签的鲁棒学习中的有效性，尤其在开放集噪声场景下表现突出。

title: WINFlowNets: Warm-up Integrated Networks Training of Generative Flow Networks for Robotics and Machine Fault Adaptation
authors: Zahin Sufiyan, Shadan Golestan, Yoshihiro Mitsuka, Shotaro Miwa, Osmar Zaiane
abstract: Generative Flow Networks for continuous scenarios (CFlowNets) have shown promise in solving sequential decision-making tasks by learning stochastic policies using a flow and a retrieval network. Despite their demonstrated efficiency compared to state-of-the-art Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithms, their practical application in robotic control tasks is constrained by the reliance on pre-training the retrieval network. This dependency poses challenges in dynamic robotic environments, where pre-training data may not be readily available or representative of the current environment. This paper introduces WINFlowNets, a novel CFlowNets framework that enables the co-training of flow and retrieval networks. WINFlowNets begins with a warm-up phase for the retrieval network to bootstrap its policy, followed by a shared training architecture and a shared replay buffer for co-training both networks. Experiments in simulated robotic environments demonstrate that WINFlowNets surpasses CFlowNets and state-of-the-art RL algorithms in terms of average reward and training stability. Furthermore, WINFlowNets exhibits strong adaptive capability in fault environments, making it suitable for tasks that demand quick adaptation with limited sample data. These findings highlight WINFlowNets' potential for deployment in dynamic and malfunction-prone robotic systems, where traditional pre-training or sample inefficient data collection may be impractical.

abstract_zh: 面向连续场景的生成流网络（CFlowNets）通过结合流网络与检索网络学习随机策略，在解决顺序决策任务中展现出潜力。尽管其效率相较于最先进的强化学习算法已有验证，但由于依赖预训练检索网络，其在机器人控制任务中的实际应用受到限制。此依赖关系在动态机器人环境中构成挑战，因为预训练数据可能难以获取或无法代表当前环境。本文提出WINFlowNets，一种 novel 的CFlowNets框架，实现了流网络与检索网络的联合训练。WINFlowNets首先通过检索网络的热身阶段启动策略，随后采用共享训练架构与共享经验回放缓冲区协同训练两个网络。在模拟机器人环境中的实验表明，WINFlowNets在平均奖励与训练稳定性方面均优于CFlowNets及最先进强化学习算法。此外，WINFlowNets在故障环境中展现出强大的自适应能力，使其适用于需以有限样本数据快速适应的任务。这些发现凸显了WINFlowNets部署于动态且易故障机器人系统中的潜力，在该类系统中，传统预训练或样本低效的数据收集方式可能并不实用。

title: SCALE:Scalable Conditional Atlas-Level Endpoint transport for virtual cell perturbation prediction
authors: Shuizhou Chen, Lang Yu, Kedu Jin, Songming Zhang, Hao Wu, Wenxuan Huang, Sheng Xu, Quan Qian, Qin Chen, Lei Bai, Siqi Sun, Zhangyang Gao
abstract: Virtual cell models aim to enable in silico experimentation by predicting how cells respond to genetic, chemical, or cytokine perturbations from single-cell measurements. In practice, however, large-scale perturbation prediction remains constrained by three coupled bottlenecks: inefficient training and inference pipelines, unstable modeling in high-dimensional sparse expression space, and evaluation protocols that overemphasize reconstruction-like accuracy while underestimating biological fidelity. In this work we present a specialized large-scale foundation model SCALE for virtual cell perturbation prediction that addresses the above limitations jointly. First, we build a BioNeMo-based training and inference framework that substantially improves data throughput, distributed scalability, and deployment efficiency, yielding 12.51* speedup on pretrain and 1.29* on inference over the prior SOTA pipeline under matched system settings. Second, we formulate perturbation prediction as conditional transport and implement it with a set-aware flow architecture that couples LLaMA-based cellular encoding with endpoint-oriented supervision. This design yields more stable training and stronger recovery of perturbation effects. Third, we evaluate the model on Tahoe-100M using a rigorous cell-level protocol centered on biologically meaningful metrics rather than reconstruction alone. On this benchmark, our model improves PDCorr by 12.02% and DE Overlap by 10.66% over STATE. Together, these results suggest that advancing virtual cells requires not only better generative objectives, but also the co-design of scalable infrastructure, stable transport modeling, and biologically faithful evaluation.

title: The Causal Uncertainty Principle: Manifold Tearing and the Topological Limits of Counterfactual Interventions
authors: Rui Wu, Hong Xie, Yongjun Li
abstract: Judea Pearl's do-calculus provides a foundation for causal inference, but its translation to continuous generative models remains fraught with geometric challenges. We establish the fundamental limits of such interventions. We define the Counterfactual Event Horizon and prove the Manifold Tearing Theorem: deterministic flows inevitably develop finite-time singularities under extreme interventions. We establish the Causal Uncertainty Principle for the trade-off between intervention extremity and identity preservation. Finally, we introduce Geometry-Aware Causal Flow (GACF), a scalable algorithm that utilizes a topological radar to bypass manifold tearing, validated on high-dimensional scRNA-seq data.

title: Causal Representation Learning on High-Dimensional Data: Benchmarks, Reproducibility, and Evaluation Metrics
authors: Alireza Sadeghi, Wael AbdAlmageed
abstract: Causal representation learning (CRL) models aim to transform high-dimensional data into a latent space, enabling interventions to generate counterfactual samples or modify existing data based on the causal relationships among latent variables. To facilitate the development and evaluation of these models, a variety of synthetic and real-world datasets have been proposed, each with distinct advantages and limitations. For practical applications, CRL models must perform robustly across multiple evaluation directions, including reconstruction, disentanglement, causal discovery, and counterfactual reasoning, using appropriate metrics for each direction. However, this multi-directional evaluation can complicate model comparison, as a model may excel in some direction while under-performing in others. Another significant challenge in this field is reproducibility: the source code corresponding to published results must be publicly available, and repeated runs should yield performance consistent with the original reports. In this study, we critically analyzed the synthetic and real-world datasets currently employed in the literature, highlighting their limitations and proposing a set of essential characteristics for suitable datasets in CRL model development. We also introduce a single aggregate metric that consolidates performance across all evaluation directions, providing a comprehensive score for each model. Finally, we reviewed existing implementations from the literature and assessed them in terms of reproducibility, identifying gaps and best practices in the field.
